ip_outcome_note: Confirmed via PokemonTCG.io swsh6-60 name field. National Pokedex no. 281. Hoenn psychic-empath mid-form with dual evolution endpoint.

trivia_bullets.text: Kirlia (National Pokedex no. 281) is classified as the 'Emotion Pokemon.' Its balletic posture is canonical: Pokedex entries note that Kirlia dances when its Trainer is in a positive mood, and its psychic power intensifies when it experiences joy or happiness. The dancing pose is an expression of psychic activation, not merely decoration.

"It has a psychic power that enables it to distort the space around it and see into the future." Space-distortion as a psychic ability is unusual framing — more Arceus-adjacent than typical Psychic-type fare — and distinguishes Kirlia from the simpler emotion-sensing role of Ralts.

'Mirage Step' searches the deck for up to 3 Kirlia and benches them simultaneously — a mass-deployment attack with 0 damage output. In the Battle Styles / Chilling Reign format this enabled rapid setup of Gardevoir Stage-2 lines. The self-targeting search (finding copies of itself) is mechanically unusual — most search attacks find evolutions forward.

This card's evolvesTo field lists both Gardevoir and Gallade — one of the few Pokemon TCG cards where a single card evolves into two different Pokemon species. The fork is gender-gated in the mainline games (male-only Gallade) but in the TCG the Gallade branch simply requires the Gallade card.

related_cards.name: Gallade
related_cards.note: final evolution, male + Dawn Stone branch
